subject: History
chapter: The Rise of Nationalism in Europe

activities_projects.title: Sorrieu’s Utopian Vision Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct the symbolism in Frederic Sorrieu’s 1848 prints
steps: Identify and circle specific symbols like the Statue of Liberty and the shattered crowns
Write a paragraph explaining why the nations are shown in a long train
Compare the flags of three nations shown in the painting with their modern counterparts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding visual representation of political ideals

activities_projects.title: Mapping the Congress of Vienna (1815)
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the territorial changes after the defeat of Napoleon
steps: Mark the territories gained by Prussia, Austria, and Russia
Draw the boundaries of the German Confederation of 39 states
Color-code the Bourbon dynasty's restoration in France
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the concept of Conservatism and the Balance of Power
activities_projects.evaluation: Geographic accuracy and map neatness (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Realpolitik' of Bismarck and Cavour
activities_projects.objective: Compare the unification processes of Germany and Italy
steps: List unique steps of German unification (Blood and Iron) in one circle
List unique steps of Italian unification (Diplomatic alliances) in the other
Fill the intersection with common features like the role of monarchy and war
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of comparison and logical points (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Allegories of the Nation: A Creative Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand how abstract ideas were personified
steps: Research the symbols associated with Germania (Oak leaves, Sword, Rays of Sun)
Create a table matching symbols with their meanings (e.g., Crown of oak leaves = Heroism)
Design a new symbol for a modern value like 'Justice' or 'Digital Equality'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding of allegories and national identity
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and accuracy of symbolism (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Greek War of Independence: A Storyboard
activities_projects.objective: Trace the role of culture and poets in nationalist movements
steps: Create a 4-panel storyboard: 1. Ottoman rule, 2. Poets like Lord Byron, 3. The 1821 struggle, 4. Treaty of Constantinople
Write captions explaining how 'Cultural Nationalism' helped the Greeks
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the impact of the Romantic movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Storytelling clarity and historical facts (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Frankfurt Parliament Debate
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the failed attempt at liberal constitutionalism
steps: Assign roles: A Liberal professional, a worker, and Friedrich Wilhelm IV
Write 3 arguments for/against the constitution drafted at St. Paul’s Church
Summarize why the parliament ultimately failed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the 1848 revolutions and their limitations

activities_projects.title: Napoleonic Code Audit
activities_projects.objective: Assess the pros and cons of the Civil Code of 1804
steps: List 3 administrative reforms introduced by Napoleon
List 3 reasons why conquered people eventually turned against French rule
Conclude whether Napoleon was a 'Liberator' or a 'Tyrant' with evidence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Ability to evaluate complex historical figures

activities_projects.title: The Balkan Tinderbox Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Identify why the Balkans became a source of tension
steps: Identify 5 modern countries that were part of the Balkan region
Write down 3 major powers that competed for control in the Balkans
Explain the link between 'Balkan Nationalism' and the start of WWI

activities_projects.title: Mazzini’s Secret Societies
activities_projects.objective: Examine the role of underground revolutionary movements
steps: Create a membership card for 'Young Italy'
Write 3 main goals of Giuseppe Mazzini’s secret societies
Explain why Metternich called him the 'most dangerous enemy of our social order'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the radical nature of the unification movement

activities_projects.title: The 'Strange Case' of Britain Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Contrast British unification with European revolutionary movements
steps: Outline the steps of the Act of Union (1707 and 1801)
Identify symbols of the new British nation (Union Jack, God Save Our Noble King)
Explain how Scottish and Irish cultures were suppressed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands state-led nationalism versus revolution-led nationalism
